procedure.title: Steps for the EWI (Equal-Width-Increment) sampling method

source.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Ensure adequate field staffing and equipment

constraints.text: field effort is adequately staffed and equipped
Preparations for water sampling are described in NFM 2 and 3

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Check QC requirements before departure

constraints.text: Check QC requirements before departing

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Set out safety equipment at field site

constraints.text: NFM 9 for field safety

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Park vehicle to prevent sample contamination

constraints.text: Park vehicle in a location and direction so as to prevent sample contamination from vehicle emissions

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Assemble sampling equipment

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Set up clean work space

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Select equipment appropriate for analyte type

constraints.text: Select equipment with fluorocarbon polymer, glass, or metal components if components will directly contact samples to be analyzed for organic compounds. Do not use plastics other than fluorocarbon polymers

Select equipment with components made of fluorocarbon polymer or other relatively inert and uncolored plastics or glass if components will directly contact samples to be analyzed for inorganic constituents. Do not use metal or rubber components for trace-element sampling
Collect samples for microbiological analyses using equipment and techniques described in NFM 7

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Visually inspect stream cross-section
steps.action_object: stream from bank to bank and longitudinally

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Observe and document velocity, width, and depth distribution

constraints.text: NFM 6 for field measurements

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Note and document stagnant water and flow anomalies
steps.action_object: stagnant water, eddies, backwater, reverse flows, and obstructions

constraints.text: Note and document the location of stagnant water, eddies, backwater, reverse flows, areas of faster than normal flow, and piers or other features along the cross section

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Determine stream width

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Measure cross-sectional field measurements
steps.action_object: field measurements (specific electrical conductance, pH, temperature, dissolved oxygen)

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Review magnitude of field measurement variations
steps.action_object: magnitude of variations along the cross section

constraints.text: Review the magnitude of the variations along the cross section

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Determine width of increment

constraints.text: Even if streamflow is divided, as in a braided channel, equal-width increments must be identical from channel to channel, and the same constant transit rate must be used at each vertical

If it is necessary to include an eddy in the cross section to be sampled, consider treating the eddy as a solid obstruction: subtract the eddy width from that of the total cross section, and determine the width of the increments based on the remaining stream width

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Calculate number of increments

constraints.text: Cross-sectional width ≥ 5 ft—use a minimum of 10 equal-width increments
Cross-sectional width < 5 ft—use as many increments as practical, but equally spaced at a minimum of 3 inches apart
When using a 1-L bottle sampler at maximum depth with a 14-L churn splitter, EWI samples can be collected at no more than 14 to 17 verticals
If an 8-L churn splitter is used, samples can be collected at no more than 10 verticals
A cone splitter must be used if the total volume collected will exceed the recommended volume for the churn splitter

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Verify increment number is whole number

constraints.text: The number of increments must be a whole number

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Adjust increment width if subsample does not represent mean value

constraints.text: If the subsample does not represent the mean value for that increment, decrease the increment width until the mean value for the increment is represented

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Locate first sampling vertical

constraints.text: Locate the first sampling vertical at a distance of one-half of the selected increment width from the edge of the water

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Locate remaining sampling verticals
steps.action_object: remaining sampling verticals at center of each increment

constraints.text: Locate all the other verticals at the center of each remaining equal-width increment along the cross section

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Make adjustments to avoid obstructions

constraints.text: Make slight adjustments to sampling locations, if necessary, to avoid sampling where the flow is affected by a pier or other obstruction

Sampling near or downstream from large instream obstructions such as bridges and piers could result in artificially elevated concentrations of suspended sediments if the sampler is immersed in an eddy that is caused by the obstruction

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Locate increment with largest discharge

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Sound for depth at maximum-discharge increment

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Measure or estimate velocity

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Determine minimum transit rate

constraints.text: Refer to Appendix A4-A for guidelines for determining the transit rates for collecting isokinetic, depth-integrated samples
The transit rate selected must be sufficiently rapid to keep from overfilling the sampler

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Approximate mean velocity using floating marker

constraints.text: Approximate the mean velocity of the vertical in feet per second by timing a floating marker (such as a peanut) as it travels a known distance
Divide the distance (in feet) by the time (in seconds) and multiply by 0.86

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Verify transit rate does not exceed maximum allowable rate
steps.action_object: transit rate against maximum allowable rate

constraints.text: Make sure that the transit rate does not exceed the maximum allowable transit rate to be used at any of the remaining verticals along the cross section

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Sample slowest increment to confirm minimum volume

constraints.text: If the minimum volume of sample (relative to depth of the vertical) is not collected at this vertical, then the EWI method cannot be used at this cross section to collect a discharge-weighted sample

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Move to first sampling vertical
steps.action_object: first vertical (midpoint of first EWI near edge of water)

constraints.text: When sampling from a bridge, deploy the sampler from the upstream of the bridge, if possible, to avoid bridge-related contamination of the sample
Use CH/DH techniques, as required (section 4.0.2)
Always follow safety procedures (NFM 9)

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Field rinse equipment

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Collect rinse water at stream edge

constraints.text: Collect the rinse water at the edge of the stream in a section of low stream velocity to minimize including suspended sediment

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Record start time and gage height

constraints.text: Record start time and gage height

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Lower sampler at constant transit rate to streambed

constraints.text: Remember that you must keep the transit rate unidirectional, constant, and within the isokinetic transit range of the sampler when collecting isokinetic samples at each centroid
The descending and ascending transit rate must be constant in each direction and must be the same for each vertical along the cross section
Do not exceed the maximum allowable transit rate if using EWI. If the transit rate must exceed the maximum allowable rate, use EDI instead of EWI
The sampler is overfilled when the water surface in the sampler container is above the bottom edge of the nozzle when the sampler is held in the sampling position
The same size sampler nozzle and container must be used at all verticals along the cross section
Lower field-rinsed sampler at the predetermined constant transit rate until slight contact is made with the streambed. Do not pause upon contacting the streambed
Do not overfill the sampler container
Do not underfill the sampler container (Appendix A4-A)
If the required volume cannot be collected, use the EDI method to obtain discharge-weighted samples

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Raise sampler at constant transit rate to complete vertical traverse

constraints.text: Raise the sampler immediately at the same constant transit rate until sampler completes the vertical traverse

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Collect sample water at each vertical increment

constraints.text: If the total volume collected will exceed the recommended volume for the churn splitter, then a cone splitter must be used

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Inspect each subsample as it is collected

constraints.text: looking for overfilling or underfilling of the sampler container and (or) the presence of anomalously large amounts of particulates that might have been captured because of excessive streambed disturbance during sample collection
If you note any of these conditions, discard the sample, making sure there are no residual particulates left in the container, and resample

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Move sampling equipment to the next vertical
steps.action_object: sampling equipment to the next vertical

constraints.text: Maintain the selected transit rate
Subsamples can be collected at several verticals before emptying the sampler container, as long as the maximum volume of sample in a bottle or bag sampler has not been exceeded
If the container is overfilled, it is necessary to resample

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Continue to the next vertical until no more samples can be collected without overfilling

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Empty the subsample into a field-rinsed churn or cone splitter and repeat collection at all verticals
steps.action_object: subsample into churn or cone splitter

constraints.text: If the total volume of the subsamples to be collected will exceed the operational capacity of the churn, select from the following options: use a smaller nozzle; or, use a cone splitter; or, use the EDI method, if appropriate
To ensure that all particulates are transferred with the sample, swirl the subsample gently to keep particulates suspended and pour the subsample quickly into the churn or cone splitter
Sample EWI verticals as many times as necessary to ensure that an adequate sample volume is collected as required for analysis, but sample at each vertical an equal number of times
If flow is stable during sampling, then multiple samples can be collected at each vertical during a single traverse along the cross section
Document on field forms the method used

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Record end-of-sampling information

constraints.text: Record the following information after all samples have been collected: Sampling end time; Ending gage height; All field observations and any deviations from standard sampling procedures

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Process samples per NFM 5

constraints.text: Refer to NFM 5

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

steps.label: Clean equipment per NFM 3

constraints.text: Refer to NFM 3

If the sampler will not be reused during a field trip, rinse sampler components with deionized water before they dry and place them into a plastic bag for transporting to the office laboratory to be cleaned

If the sampler will be reused during the field trip, rinse the components with DIW while still wet from sampling and then field-clean while at the sampling site using the prescribed procedures (NFM 3). Reassemble the sampler
Collect a field blank, if required, after sampling equipment has been cleaned at the sampling site
Place the cleaned sampler into a plastic bag and seal for transport to the next site

provenance.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method

[verbatim-grounding + completion pass 2026-07-06, agent, pending human sign-off] char_end 91092 -> 95437: the old span cut off mid-Step 4c, so Steps 4d-4g and the Step 6 cleaning bullets were never annotated ('take the whole unit' scope rule). Added S35 (inspect subsample, C49-C50), S36 (move to next vertical, C51-C53), S37 (continue until overfill limit), S38 (empty into churn/cone + repeat, C54-C56, C65-C66), S39 (record end-of-sampling info, C57); old S35/S36 renumbered S40/S41 (adjudication log above refers to the old numbering). Added S32/S33 traverse guards C62-C64 (do-not-overfill, do-not-underfill, EDI fallback) and S41 cleaning constraints C58-C61. Text re-grounds: C22 restored 'at this vertical' (had been substituted) and retyped guard; C23 restored full 'Remember that you must... at each centroid'; C20 restored '(such as a peanut)' with the divide-multiply formula split into C48 (its old text spliced across a skipped sentence); C46 restored 'that is'; C37/C38 -> verbatim 'Refer to NFM 5'/'Refer to NFM 3'; C41/C44 -> verbatim footnote fragments (invented 'Consult' prefixes removed). Dropped C43 (NFM 8 bottom-material reference — no annotated step it governs). OCR hyphen/space repairs in C3/C4/C10/C21 retained per guidelines. Deferred to human: the Appendix A4-A round-trip-transit TECHNICAL NOTE after Step 4e (possible reference constraint on S36/S24). 45 -> 63 constraints, 36 -> 41 steps.
step:S37 zero_constraints adjudicated; step:S8 zero_constraints adjudicated (C43 NFM-8 reference dropped as ungoverned; S8 inspection qualifiers live in S9/S10 constraints)
[relations pass 2026-07-06, agent, pending human sign-off] Step-graph relations annotated by hand: NEXT = document-order procedural succession; fork NEXT edges plus ALTERNATIVE_TO only where the source states mutually exclusive branches. Conditional in-step behaviour stays encoded as guard constraints. Loops (check-measurement repeat, per-vertical sampling) not modeled to keep the graph acyclic. NOTE: current Tier-B eval synthesizes a linear NEXT chain from steps-array order and does not read this field; relations are provided as explicit gold ordering/branching for graph consumers and export.
